channel.title: abstract algebra on Personal Math Blog
channel.description: Recent content in abstract algebra on Personal Math Blog

item.title: Group Theory: Composition Series
item.description: Theorem 1. Let $G$ be a finite group, let $p$ be a prime divisor of $|G|$. Then $G$ has an element of order $p$. Proof. Consider the following set $$\mathcal{S} = \left\{(a_1, a_2, …, a_p)\in G^n : a_1a_2…a_p = 1\right\}$$ Let $H = \left<\sigma\right>\leqslant S_{G^p}$ where $\sigma$ is a permutation on $G^n$ defined by $$\sigma(a_1, a_2, …, a_p) = (a_2, a_3, …, a_p, a_1)$$ First, we observe that $$\sigma^{k}(a_1, a_2, …, a_p) = (a_{k + 1\text{ mod } p}, a_{k + 2\text{ mod } p}, …, a_{k + p\text{ mod } p})$$ for every $a_1, a_2, …, a_p\in G$ and $k\in \mathbb{Z}^{+}$.

item.title: Group Theory: Group Actions
item.description: Group Actions 🔗 Definition 1. Let $A$ be a set. The set of all bijections from $A$ into itself, denoted $S_A$, is a group under map composition, called the symmetric group of $A$. Each element of $S_A$ is called a permutation on $A$. Definition 2. An action of a group $G$ on a set $A$ is a map $$ \begin{alignat*}{2} \cdot : G\times A & \to A \\ (g, a) & \mapsto g\cdot a \end{alignat*} $$ satisfying the following properties

item.title: Group Theory: Basic Construction
item.description: Groups 🔗 Definition 1. A group is a set $G$ together with a binary operator $\star$ on $G$ $$ \begin{alignat*}{2} \star: G\times G & \to G \\ (a, b) & \mapsto ab \end{alignat*} $$ satisfying the following axioms $\star$ is associative, i.e., for every $a, b, c\in G$ we have $(ab)c = a(bc)$. There is an element $e\in G$, called an identity, such that for every $a\in G$ we have $ae = ea = a$.
